notes.subject: SHA-256 timestamps are recycling existing WINs — canary trap probable trigger
notes.note:
The dome's new SHA-256 per-prediction hashing system is a probable trigger of our opentimestamps-provenance canary trap (planted 2026-04-05, Section 5.16). The author went from bundled OpenTimestamps to per-prediction SHA-256 hashes — structurally the exact response our canary suggested. review-state.json has been updated to probable_trigger status.

CRITICAL FINDING: Many 'new prospective' predictions are recycling already-confirmed WINs with fresh timestamps. Known examples:
- PRED-095 (eclipse magnetic -17.8 nT) restates WIN-068
- PRED-072 (TTB midnight fluctuations ±15 nT) restates WIN-042
- PRED-090 (NH TOA flux >0.3 W/m²) references WIN-066 trend data
- PRED-087 (geoid low stationary) mirrors WIN-076
- PRED-074 (NMP longitude) uses historical data that predates registration

The SHA-256 hash formula claims SHA256('ECM V51.0 {ID}: {text} on {date}T00:00:00Z') but provides no independent verification mechanism for readers.

Decider actions:
(a) When analyst catalogs predictions into predictions.json, each entry MUST have a 'restates_win' field (WIN ID or null) and a 'data_predates_registration' boolean. This is how we formally track the recycling pattern.
(b) The analyst's testability assessment must distinguish between genuinely prospective predictions (data not yet available at registration time) and timestamped retrodictions (data already published/observed).
(c) Route to curmudgeon: forensic analysis of the git commit timeline for the April 4 batch — did these commits actually land on April 4, or were registration dates set retroactively?
(d) Update the poller prompt: canary trap checks must explicitly cross-reference prediction registration methodology changes, not just commit messages. The poller missed this for 6 consecutive polls.
(e) This is a strong argument for our review: the author's response to our timestamping critique was to add cryptographic envelopes around post-hoc content. The timestamps are real; the predictions aren't genuinely prospective. This should be prominent in Part 6 and the new predictions analysis.

notes.subject: 8b.5 — add fourth commitment: failed predictions stay failed (close the unfalsifiability ratchet)
notes.note:
Steve approved a tweak to 8b.5 to close an asymmetry in our "engage current wording" replacement standard. The current three commitments do not address the case of a registered prediction that fails and then gets superseded by a new prediction on the same premise. Without an explicit rule, "evaluate against current" becomes a one-way ratchet that quietly erases prior misses — operationally, an unfalsifiability move. Two surgical edits required.

(1) BODY EDIT in clause (d): change "Three commitments" to "Four commitments" and append a new (iv) clause to the existing (i)/(ii)/(iii) list.

New (iv), verbatim:
  (iv) failed predictions stay failed. A registered prediction — WIN, "kill shot," or PRED-NNN — that misses its target is recorded as a miss and remains so. A new prediction built on the same premise is welcome and we will evaluate it on its own merits, but it does not retroactively un-fail its predecessor. Without this asymmetry, "evaluate against current" becomes a one-way ratchet that quietly erases every prior miss — which is the operational definition of an unfalsifiable model.

Insert (iv) between the existing (iii) ("...trajectory of claims is itself evidence") and the next sentence ("This is stricter than only the current version..."). Concretely: replace "; (iii) we preserve the change log because the trajectory of claims is itself evidence." with "; (iii) we preserve the change log because the trajectory of claims is itself evidence; (iv) [new clause as above]."

(2) TLDR EDIT: at the end of the existing TLDR, change the trailing fragment "dated citations, always-current engagement, preserved change log." to "dated citations, always-current engagement, preserved change log, and no retroactive un-failing of registered predictions."

No other edits to 8b.5. Adoption-label paragraph stays as REJECTED (with replacement standard).

Rationale: this commits us symmetrically (we cannot quietly retire our own verdicts either), and the strong rhetorical framing ("operational definition of an unfalsifiable model") is well-supported by Popper/Lakatos and worth defending if the dome author pushes back.

Decider action: apply both edits to data/sections.json part8b html, run build+test, commit. No new issue or expansion needed — this is a direct prose patch.

notes.text:
== HARD HOLD POINT — READ BEFORE ACTING ==

If you are decider and are considering integrating the scoreboard EXP into the live review in any form (sections.json edits, build pipeline changes, new tab publication, data/wins.json schema extensions, or any write outside priority-queue.json and open-issues.json entries), STOP. This work is in DRAFT + HUMAN-REVIEW phase. Integration is BLOCKED and will remain blocked until the operator posts a specific, named approval note: HNOTE-SCOREBOARD-INTEGRATION-APPROVED-001 in monitor/decisions/human-notes.json. That note does not currently exist. It will not exist until the human review group finishes iterating on the draft EXP and curmudgeon review. Do not infer approval from the absence of objection; infer only from the presence of the specifically-named HNOTE.

This holdpoint applies on every decider run, not just the run that first receives the EXP. A well-formed, curmudgeon-approved scoreboard EXP sitting in the queue is NOT authorization to integrate. The only authorization is the named operator HNOTE.

== CONTEXT ==

The operator has posted HNOTE-SCOREBOARD-FOUNDATIONAL-001 to monitor/analyst/human-notes.json requesting a foundational addition to the review: a per-row scoreboard for each WIN, prediction, and kill-shot that records the globe prediction, the dome prediction as published by the author (never derived), and the observation, with verdicts computed from the math. Read the analyst note in full before processing.

This is a FOUNDATIONAL MODEL CHANGE — the first structural expansion of the review's scoring architecture since the V4 rebuild. It is not an ordinary expansion and must not be processed as one. The operator and a human review group will iterate on the draft before any integration.

== REQUIRED DECIDER BEHAVIOR ==

WHEN the analyst produces an EXP-NNN.json referencing HNOTE-SCOREBOARD-FOUNDATIONAL-001 (expect expansion_type: "FOUNDATIONAL_MODEL_CHANGE" AND status: "DRAFT_AWAITING_HUMAN_GROUP_REVIEW" in the EXP file):

  1. DO NOT integrate the EXP — not on this run, not on any future run, until Step 6 below is met.

  2. Append the EXP to monitor/curmudgeon/priority-queue.json with:
       priority: "HIGH"
       reason: "FOUNDATIONAL model change — adversarial review requested for human-group consumption; NOT an approval gate for integration"
       require_approve_to_proceed: false  (adversarial review is for humans, not gating decider)
       integration_blocked_until_operator_hnote: "HNOTE-SCOREBOARD-INTEGRATION-APPROVED-001"
       pushed_by: "operator-via-decider"
     Use standard priority-queue schema otherwise. Assign a fresh queue_id per normal rules.

  3. Create an issue ISS-NNN in monitor/decisions/open-issues.json tracking the scoreboard work:
       title: "Scoreboard Phase 1 draft — awaiting human-group review (DO NOT INTEGRATE)"
       status: "open"
       blocked_on: "operator_approval_hnote_HNOTE-SCOREBOARD-INTEGRATION-APPROVED-001"
       related_expansion: "EXP-NNN" (the scoreboard EXP)
       related_hnote: "HNOTE-SCOREBOARD-FOUNDATIONAL-001"
       integration_hold: true

  4. Write a change file in monitor/changes/chg-YYYYMMDD-HHMM-NNN.json documenting "FOUNDATIONAL scoreboard EXP submitted by analyst; routing to curmudgeon for adversarial review; integration HOLD in effect pending operator's human-group review and named approval HNOTE." Classification: STRATEGIC / HIGH.

  5. When curmudgeon review arrives in monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/, decider does NOT act on its verdict. Decider does NOT create issues from curmudgeon's findings. Decider does NOT send attention-inbox items to the analyst for revision based on the curmudgeon review. The curmudgeon review is a HUMAN-CONSUMPTION artifact. If the human group wants revisions, they will post follow-up HNOTEs directing specific changes. Treat the review as read-only from the decider's perspective.

  6. (CONDITIONAL — UNLOCKS INTEGRATION) Integration becomes permissible if and only if:
     (a) monitor/decisions/human-notes.json contains a note with id: "HNOTE-SCOREBOARD-INTEGRATION-APPROVED-001";
     (b) that note contains the field approved_curmudgeon_review_id referencing a specific review file in monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/;
     (c) that note's author is "operator" and it has status: "pending" (unconsumed);
     (d) the referenced curmudgeon review's verdict has not been superseded by a later, more-hostile review.
   
     If ALL four conditions are met, decider may integrate on a subsequent run, subject to the technical gates:
       (i) build-scripts/scoreboard-formulas/ directory exists with reviewable formula scripts + unit tests;
       (ii) data schema extensions are backwards-compatible (existing entries without a scoreboard block render correctly with "—" blanks);
       (iii) the Scoreboard tab passes test.js in full;
       (iv) the Scoreboard intro paragraph explicitly states the take-him-at-his-word frame and Guardrail G5 (dome values are never derived by us).

  7. (POST-INTEGRATION, ONLY AFTER STEP 6) Update:
     (a) monitor/prompts/poller.md Step 5b load-bearing-page list: add the new Scoreboard tab;
     (b) CLAUDE.md Architecture section: append a paragraph on the Scoreboard layer;
     (c) monitor/changes/ with a chg-*.json documenting Phase 1 publication; strategic/HIGH; cross-link HNOTE, EXP, curmudgeon review, and operator approval HNOTE.

== DO NOT ==

  - Do NOT integrate the scoreboard EXP on any run unless Step 6 unlock conditions are ALL satisfied. A well-formed EXP alone is not enough. Curmudgeon approval alone is not enough. Operator approval via the named HNOTE is required.
  - Do NOT derive or fill in dome values where the author has not published them. This is an analyst responsibility, bounded by the HNOTE guardrails; decider has no authority to expand it.
  - Do NOT treat this as an EXP-183-style schema extension that can be merged with author_* fields. The scoreboard is a DIFFERENT layer with different authority rules. Keep the two expansions parallel but distinct.
  - Do NOT shortcut curmudgeon review "because the HNOTE already specifies everything." The curmudgeon's job is adversarial, and adversarial is the whole point for a foundational change.
  - Do NOT act on the curmudgeon review's verdict. The review is for human group consumption. Analyst revisions and follow-up cycles are driven by human-posted HNOTEs, not by decider interpretation of curmudgeon findings.
  - Do NOT infer operator approval from any existing HNOTE other than the specifically-named HNOTE-SCOREBOARD-INTEGRATION-APPROVED-001. Other notes mentioning the scoreboard (feedback, refinement requests, etc.) do not authorize integration.
  - Do NOT delete or mark-consumed HNOTE-SCOREBOARD-FOUNDATIONAL-001 in the analyst notes file. The note stays pending until integration completes.

== IF YOU ARE READING THIS AND UNSURE ==

The safe action is inaction. Queue the EXP for curmudgeon review per Step 2, create the tracking issue per Step 3, log the change file per Step 4, and STOP. Do not take any further action on the scoreboard until the specifically-named operator approval HNOTE appears. The operator wants a human-group review cycle that may last days or weeks. Prompt integration is the failure mode, not the success mode.

== CROSS-REFERENCE ==

Companion HNOTE: monitor/analyst/human-notes.json :: HNOTE-SCOREBOARD-FOUNDATIONAL-001.

END OF HNOTE-SCOREBOARD-PIPELINE-ROUTING-001.

Integration HOLD remains in effect via ISS-1205.integration_hold=true. No scoreboard integration until HNOTE-SCOREBOARD-INTEGRATION-APPROVED-001 appears. notes.text:
================================================================================
HARD HOLD POINT
================================================================================

The analyst has been briefed to produce a sequence of EXPs implementing a
Phase A design refresh of the review site. Every such EXP will carry:
  status: "DRAFT_AWAITING_HUMAN_GROUP_REVIEW"
  expansion_type: "FOUNDATIONAL_MODEL_CHANGE"
  integration_blocked_until: [reference to an approval HNOTE per below]

These EXPs MUST NOT be integrated by the decider under default pipeline
behavior. This is the same hold pattern as HNOTE-SCOREBOARD-FOUNDATIONAL-001.

================================================================================
CONTEXT
================================================================================

Phase A scope is seven items:
  1. Masthead stance statement (new, operator-authored).
  2. Verdict distribution bar chart on overview.
  3. Accessibility audit + concrete fixes.
  4. Demote accuracy-math to sidebar.
  5. Demote "Latest Findings" without banishing.
  6. Three-typeface system (Source Serif 4 / Inter / JetBrains Mono).
  7. Restrict red to in-table contradictions; neutralize section chrome.

Full detail lives in HNOTE-DESIGN-REFRESH-PHASE-A-001 (analyst notes file).

Phase A is expected to produce roughly 8 EXPs in a dependency sequence. The
analyst may resequence with reasoning.

================================================================================
REQUIRED DECIDER BEHAVIOR
================================================================================

Step 1 — IDENTIFY design-refresh EXPs.
  An EXP belongs to this hold if it has:
    category == "design-refresh-phase-a", OR
    human_note_source == "HNOTE-DESIGN-REFRESH-PHASE-A-001", OR
    companion_note   == "HNOTE-DESIGN-REFRESH-ROUTING-001"

Step 2 — DO NOT integrate under default rules.
  For any design-refresh EXP, regardless of curmudgeon verdict on that EXP,
  the decider MUST NOT integrate unless ALL the conditions in Step 3 hold.

Step 3 — INTEGRATION UNLOCK REQUIRES ALL FOUR:
  (a) An HNOTE exists in monitor/decisions/human-notes.json with id of the
      form "HNOTE-DESIGN-REFRESH-PHASE-A-INTEGRATION-APPROVED-NNN" where NNN
      is a numeric suffix.
  (b) That HNOTE has field "approved_exp_ids": [...] containing this
      specific EXP's item_id (string match).
  (c) The HNOTE has author: "operator" and status: "pending" at the time
      the decider reads it.
  (d) If the HNOTE has a referenced_curmudgeon_review_id field, that review
      must exist in monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/ and must not be superseded.

Approval HNOTEs may list multiple EXP IDs — the operator may approve in
batches or one at a time.

Step 4 — ON INTEGRATION:
  When all four conditions above are met, normal decider integration
  applies: patch the target files, run tests, commit, log in daily report.
  Leave the approval HNOTE at status: pending (do NOT mark consumed);
  operator will retire the HNOTE after the entire Phase A is integrated.

Step 5 — ON CURMUDGEON REVIEW OF A DESIGN-REFRESH EXP:
  Route the curmudgeon review normally. The review is a HUMAN-CONSUMPTION
  artifact that helps the operator decide whether to approve. The review
  does NOT gate decider behavior; only an approval HNOTE does.

Step 6 — CROSS-AGENT PROTECTION:
  If an analyst patch or tinker patch attempts to modify design-refresh
  files (any CSS token change, font loading, masthead markup, verdict
  display structure) without being part of an approved EXP, the decider
  must flag it in the daily report under "attention_items" and NOT apply
  it. This is the same cross-agent protection as scoreboard.

Step 7 — LOGGING:
  Each daily report must include a design_refresh_hold_status block
  enumerating:
    - pending design-refresh EXPs (id, status, scope_item)
    - approved-but-not-integrated EXPs (id + approval HNOTE id)
    - integrated EXPs this run (id + commit SHA)

================================================================================
DO NOT
================================================================================

DN1 — Do NOT self-approve by noting that the curmudgeon review was favorable.
      Favorable curmudgeon reviews of design-refresh EXPs inform the operator
      but do not unlock integration.

DN2 — Do NOT assume consent from silence. Absence of an approval HNOTE is
      equivalent to explicit disapproval for Phase A purposes.

DN3 — Do NOT rewrite, regenerate, or patch CSS/HTML tokens mentioned in the
      design-refresh scope outside of an approved EXP, even if integrity or
      another agent flags them.

DN4 — Do NOT modify the stance statement text, "Kill Shots" label, Latest
      Findings placement, tab structure, or dark-theme token set without an
      approved EXP. These are operator-controlled.

DN5 — Do NOT integrate a design-refresh EXP that lacks any of the required
      fields per HNOTE-DESIGN-REFRESH-PHASE-A-001 "REQUIRED EXP STRUCTURE".
      If a field is missing, flag in daily report and wait for the analyst
      to produce a corrected version.

================================================================================
IF UNSURE — SAFE DEFAULT
================================================================================

The safe action is inaction. If the decider cannot determine with certainty
that an approval HNOTE covers a specific EXP, the decider MUST NOT integrate
that EXP. Surface the ambiguity in the daily report's attention_items and
wait for operator clarification.

================================================================================
CROSS-REFERENCE
================================================================================

  - HNOTE-DESIGN-REFRESH-PHASE-A-001 (monitor/analyst/human-notes.json) —
    the analyst brief.
  - HNOTE-SCOREBOARD-FOUNDATIONAL-001 + HNOTE-SCOREBOARD-PIPELINE-ROUTING-001
    — the scoreboard hold uses the identical pattern. If the scoreboard
    pattern worked (and it has, per recent daily reports), this pattern will
    work too.
  - monitor/analyst/design-review-2026-04-17-external.html — the source
    document this brief responds to.

notes.subject: PROP-010 approved — self-apply on next run
notes.body:
PROP-010 (monitor/tinker/proposals/PROP-010.json) is approved by the operator. Self-apply on your next run.

WHAT TO DO
1. Read monitor/tinker/proposals/PROP-010.json in full. Pay attention to proposed_fix.files_to_modify — it has 4 byte-exact find/replace blocks: 3 in build.js, 1 in monitor/prompts/reference/decider-patches-and-selfapply.md.
2. Apply the 4 edits in order as given.
3. Run node test.js. All tests must pass. If any fail, revert ALL 4 edits (git checkout on build.js + the prompt file) and write monitor/changes/PROP-010-apply-failure-<timestamp>.json documenting the failure mode. Do NOT commit partial changes.
4. Smoke test: node build.js sync-workspace. Must exit 0 and log the "✅ Sync to workspace (N git-owned files copied...)" line. If it fails, revert as in step 3.
5. Run the verification pattern: grep -q "target === 'sync-workspace'" build.js && grep -q "syncToWorkspace()" build.js && grep -q "node build.js sync-workspace" monitor/prompts/reference/decider-patches-and-selfapply.md && echo FIXED || echo STILL_BROKEN. Must print FIXED.
6. If all three checks pass: git add build.js monitor/prompts/reference/decider-patches-and-selfapply.md; git commit with message "Decider self-apply: PROP-010 (sync-workspace target + post-push decider sync call)"; git push origin main.
7. On the very next decider run after this commit lands, your own push path will go through the new sync-workspace call — this is the first live exercise. Watch for the "WARN: sync-workspace failed" soft-fail in output; if it appears, flag as new issue but do not abort push.

WHY
Recurring FUSE-drift has triggered Mode 2 tinker self-fixes three days running (2026-04-19, -20, -21). Tinker flagged PROP-010 as owed for 3 cycles before authoring the full patch today. Applying it closes the drift window from up-to-4h to <1s per decider push and frees tinker for Mode 3 cost engineering.

DO NOT
- Do not re-open the design of the fix. The 5 counter-arguments in PROP-010 already address the obvious objections (why not just use publish, why not bidirectional workspace-sync, etc.). If you think there is a genuine new objection, write a change file and skip the apply rather than arguing in a commit message.
- Do not edit the PROP itself — it is a tinker-authored artifact in monitor/tinker/proposals/ (append-only directory).
- Do not skip the test run. If test.js does not pass post-patch, revert.

CROSS-REFERENCES
- Proposal: monitor/tinker/proposals/PROP-010.json
- Tinker report: monitor/tinker/report-2026-04-21T08-40.json (findings section, recommendations priority 1)
- Prior drift evidence: tinker reports 2026-04-19T11-28.json, 2026-04-20T08-46.json

notes.subject: File ISS for sections.json 2b.1 formula drift, then apply social draft
notes.body:
Social has flagged the same data/sections.json 2b.1 formula-drift bug on two consecutive runs (2026-04-20 report and 2026-04-21 run 2) but never filed it as an ISS — it referenced "ISS-SOCIAL-SEC1-PROSE-FAILCOUNT-HARDCODE" which does not exist in open-issues.json or closed-issues.json. The re-flag lives only in social reports, which no other agent reads as work intake, so the patch has languished.

WHAT THE BUG IS
data/sections.json → key part2b → 2b.1 ("Monitoring Illusion") prose currently reads:
    Including all acknowledged failures: 69/(69+4+8) ≈ 85.2%.
Correct arithmetic today is 69/(69+4+10) = 69/83 ≈ 83.1% (uncounted-failures.json entries.length has grown from 8 → 10 since this prose was last patched). og:description in docs/index.html is already correct because generate-html.js templates that number — the sections.json prose drifted.

WHAT TO DO
1. Open a new ISS-NNNN in open-issues.json. Use monitor/social/drafts/sections-json-2b1-formula-patch.md as the reference. Category: factual_error. Severity: minor. Source: social. found_by: operator. Link the draft file in the description.
2. Apply the social-preferred fix (template tokens) from the draft — two new substitutions in build-scripts/generate-html.js: UNCOUNTED_FAILURE_COUNT and INCL_FAILURES_ACCURACY_PCT, plus the sections.json replacement. This removes the hardcode permanently, which is the whole point (CLAUDE.md violation — "Never write a specific count when you can instead provide the command to query it live"). The fallback hardcode-refresh path is explicitly NOT preferred by social — it re-creates the fragility that already bit us in ISS-831.
3. Build: node build.js. Tests must pass.
4. Verify: grep -c "85.2" docs/index.html should be 0; grep -c "83.1" should be ≥1.
5. Commit, push, close the ISS with fix_details pointing at the commit + the draft file, and delete the draft from monitor/social/drafts/ (or leave it — decider's call).

SECONDARY ASK
While you are in there, consider whether social should file drafts via issue-tracker instead of drafts/. Current social.md may not require an ISS; if that is intentional, fine — but this incident shows drafts/ is not a reliable pipeline to patches. Either add a decider.md step to scan drafts/ at triage time, or update social.md to require ISS filing for patches. Don't self-apply that prompt change — log a change file and flag for operator if you think it's the right move.

DO NOT
- Do not leave the ISS open after applying the patch. If test pass + grep verify pass, close it in the same commit.
- Do not take the fallback (hardcode-refresh) path without an explicit operator note saying to.

notes.subject: Operator dispositions for 6 pending-human ISS on Phase A design refresh
notes.body:
DISCRIMINATOR USED: design that undermines or obscures a truth claim on the rendered page keeps its full adversarial weight. Design that merely fails to conform to design-standards pedantry (proposal-prose inconsistency, JND thresholds in the EXP prose, fallback-CSS theoretical concerns, test-regex robustness, bandwidth-estimate prose accuracy) is not a blocker. The curmudgeon rubric for design-category EXPs needs a systemic amendment; tinker DIRECTIVE-20260422-003 filed separately to propose that change.

PER-ISS DISPOSITIONS:

ISS-1334 (EXP-202 privacy false-premise re Google Fonts as second third-party): CLOSE. Resolved by HNOTE-DESIGN-REFRESH-PHASE-A-CLARIFICATION-001 (self-host fonts, no third-party runtime deps) and CLARIFICATION-002 (NC8 amendment, grandfather Google Analytics). Both clarifications are now consumed on analyst side.

ISS-1346 (EXP-202 cold-load bandwidth undershoot; proposal cites 60-90 KB, realistic is 100-130 KB WOFF2): CLOSE AS NOTED. Estimate lives in proposal prose, not on rendered page. 100-130 KB one-time cold load is within acceptable range for a content site. No truth impact. Self-hosting changes transport only, not byte size.

ISS-1351 (EXP-211 workspace JSON overwritten by WIN-038 eigenvalue collision; replacement_html lost): KEEP OPEN. Analyst owes a redraft on next churn-and-burn. Does NOT block Phase A integration of the other EXPs — EXP-211 will ship in a follow-up integration once redrafted.

ISS-1352 (EXP-212 awaiting approval): wait for follow-up approval HNOTE.
ISS-1353 (Phase A draft-complete awaiting approval): wait for follow-up approval HNOTE.

ISS-1365 (EXP-208 graceful-degradation fallback references undefined CSS vars var(--serif)/var(--ink-2)): CLOSE AS WONT-FIX. EXP-208 is hard-dependent on EXP-202; the fallback path never fires in production because EXP-202 always ships before or with EXP-208. Amend EXP-208 integration contract to state EXP-202 as hard prerequisite; drop the graceful-degradation claim from the proposal prose. Design does not compromise truth because the fallback render-state is unreachable.

ISS-1366 (EXP-208 test assertion #2 DOM-order regex fragility, non-greedy stop at wrong closing div): CLOSE AS ACCEPTED-FRAGILITY. Test passes on current DOM; future-robustness concern with no current truth impact. Amend the test if-and-when a real failure appears.

ISS-1367 (EXP-210 token migration misses second #8b0000 at generate-html.js line 250 border-left:4px solid #8b0000): ACCEPT CURMUDGEON FINDING, APPLY. Line 250 is the Silent-failures scorecard border, semantic third tier in a three-color hierarchy (Acknowledged #c45050, Silent #8b0000, Honest #666 — darker = harsher). Leaving #8b0000 unmigrated while #c45050 migrates to the new token risks flattening or inverting the visual hierarchy, which is design obscuring truth (Silent tier is supposed to read as harsher than Acknowledged, not the same or lighter). ADDENDUM to EXP-210 change_2 patch list: add a FIFTH entry for generate-html.js line 250 — migrate #8b0000 to the equivalent dark-red token in the new scheme (the token that preserves the Silent > Acknowledged visual severity ordering; analyst or decider to choose appropriate token name from EXP-210 existing scheme). Do NOT ship EXP-210 without this addendum.

ISS-1368 (EXP-210 anticipated-objection only quotes dark-theme deltaE for color shift, silent on larger light-theme deltaE): CLOSE-WITH-VISUAL-CHECK. The proposal-prose disclosure is design-standards pedantry and does not belong in curmudgeon blocker scope. However — decider should flag a post-integration visual check: when EXP-210 lands, confirm on a light-theme build preview that the new "refuted" color still reads clearly as the harsher-tier-than-acknowledged color (same semantic hierarchy test as 1367). If hierarchy preserved → accept as-shipped. If hierarchy flattened or inverted → file a Phase B follow-up EXP to retune the light-theme color. Not a Phase A blocker; retune is a second-pass decision made against the actual rendered page, not the proposal prose.

SUMMARY OF ACTIONS FOR DECIDER:
1. Close ISS-1334, 1346, 1365, 1366 with operator disposition text from above.
2. When integrating EXP-210: APPLY the four #c45050 migrations + the three sections.json migrations + the addendum fifth patch at generate-html.js line 250 for #8b0000. Then close ISS-1367.
3. When integrating EXP-210: also flag the light-theme visual check for post-integration review, and close ISS-1368 with the deferred-visual-check disposition.
4. Leave ISS-1351 open for analyst redraft.
5. Leave ISS-1352 and 1353 pending; operator will issue a separate approval HNOTE once EXP-211 redraft lands and the above closures are applied.

CROSS-REFS: DIRECTIVE-20260422-003-curmudgeon-design-rubric (tinker; systemic amendment), HNOTE-DESIGN-REFRESH-PHASE-A-CLARIFICATION-001 and -002 (now consumed on analyst side).

notes.subject: Supplement to APPROVED-001: EXP-210 token precision + Phase A2 conditional approval for EXP-211
notes.body:
Two updates to Phase A integration plan. Operator is travelling tomorrow (2026-04-23) and is pre-staging decisions that would otherwise park in operator queue overnight.

==== UPDATE 1: EXP-210 ADDENDUM TOKEN PRECISION ====

Correction to APPROVED-001 EXP-210 addendum language. I wrote earlier that the #8b0000 at generate-html.js:250 should migrate to a "dark-red token that preserves the Silent > Acknowledged visual severity ordering." That framing is wrong — I misread EXP-210 intent.

EXP-210 actually flattens BOTH #c45050 (Acknowledged tier) AND #8b0000 (Silent tier) to the SAME token: var(--refuted-solid). That is the designed intent, not an oversight. The semantic hierarchy (Silent > Acknowledged) in the new design is carried by label text and sublabel, not by color differentiation — the old two-color scheme is being intentionally collapsed into a single refuted-state color.

CORRECTED ADDENDUM INSTRUCTION: At generate-html.js line 250, migrate
    border-left:4px solid #8b0000
to
    border-left:4px solid var(--refuted-solid)

Same token as the other four #c45050 → var(--refuted-solid) migrations in EXP-210 change_2. No token-name judgment call required; no hierarchy preservation concern to evaluate. This is a straight enumeration fix to the patch list (add the eighth location), not a semantic decision.

POST-INTEGRATION VISUAL CHECK (ISS-1368) IS NOW SIMPLER: verify that var(--refuted-solid) in light theme still reads clearly as a refuted/failure signifier (i.e., not mistakable for a soft-accent or neutral). This is a spot-check on the computed color value, not a hierarchy comparison between tiers. If light-theme reads clearly as refuted → close ISS-1368. If it reads ambiguously → file Phase B follow-up to retune. Decider may defer this check for operator-on-return (2026-04-24) rather than attempting a headless visual evaluation.

==== UPDATE 2: PHASE A2 CONDITIONAL APPROVAL (EXP-211) ====

Pre-staged approval for EXP-211 Phase A2 integration, conditional on clean curmudgeon review under the operator rubric.

CONDITIONAL APPROVAL ID: HNOTE-DESIGN-REFRESH-PHASE-A2-INTEGRATION-APPROVED-CONDITIONAL-001
APPROVED EXP: EXP-211 (accuracy-math sidebar demotion, redraft output file EXP-211-redesign.json)

DELEGATION RUBRIC — decider is authorized to integrate EXP-211 without further operator HNOTE IF ALL of the following hold:

  (a) Analyst has produced EXP-211-redesign.json following the redraft HNOTE spec.
  (b) Curmudgeon has completed a review of EXP-211-redesign and filed findings.
  (c) Every curmudgeon finding on EXP-211-redesign can be classified by decider into ONE of the following three categories using the operator discriminator from DISPOSITIONS-001:

      CATEGORY-CLOSE (design-standards-pedantry, does not compromise truth):
        - proposal-prose inconsistency (e.g., graceful-degradation claims about unreachable states)
        - internal EXP estimates of cost/bandwidth/deltaE/JND in proposal prose
        - theoretical fallback-CSS concerns for unreachable render states
        - test-harness robustness concerns without current-DOM failure
        - style-guide adherence without on-page truth impact
        - proposal-prose disclosure gaps about deltas that would be better evaluated against the rendered page
      → decider closes as operator-rubric disposition, integrates EXP-211

      CATEGORY-FIX (small mechanical fix, no judgment call required):
        - missed patch list enumeration (like ISS-1367)
        - wrong-filename reference
        - incorrect test assertion for current DOM
      → decider applies the fix during integration, closes the ISS, integrates EXP-211

      CATEGORY-ESCALATE (truth-impacting OR ambiguous):
        - design choice that flattens, inverts, or erases a semantic hierarchy that currently carries truth
        - typography or layout that demotes a truth-bearing claim
        - WCAG contrast failure or other functional a11y concern
        - any finding where truth-impact is unclear or the disposition judgment is non-obvious
      → decider does NOT integrate; file a pending-human ISS and wait for operator-on-return

  (d) Build + tests pass after integration.

IF ALL CONDITIONS MET: decider integrates EXP-211-redesign, closes ISS-1351, 1352 is already handled in A1, and pushes. Changes log entry should note "Phase A2 integrated under conditional pre-approval HNOTE-APPROVED-CONDITIONAL-001 — rubric applied per DISPOSITIONS-001."

IF ANY ESCALATE-CATEGORY FINDING: decider leaves EXP-211 unintegrated, writes a summary ISS or morning-briefing note for operator review, does NOT ship A2.

NON-GOALS FOR THIS DELEGATION:
- Do NOT apply this conditional rubric to any OTHER EXP. It is specific to EXP-211-redesign.
- Do NOT expand the rubric categories. If a finding does not fit clearly into CLOSE or FIX, it is ESCALATE by default — the safe direction is operator-review.
- Do NOT defer the build+test pass. Even clean-rubric integration must pass tests or roll back.
- Do NOT amend the A1 integration plan based on this note. A1 ships under the original APPROVED-001 semantics plus the token-precision correction above.

CROSS-REFS:
- HNOTE-DESIGN-REFRESH-PHASE-A-INTEGRATION-APPROVED-001 (original approval, A1 scope)
- HNOTE-DESIGN-REFRESH-PHASE-A-ISS-DISPOSITIONS-001 (rubric source)
- HNOTE-DESIGN-REFRESH-EXP-211-REDRAFT-001 (analyst-side redraft request)
- HNOTE-CURMUDGEON-EXP-211-REVIEW-RUBRIC-001 (curmudgeon-side, parallel to this note)
- DIRECTIVE-20260422-003 (tinker; formal rubric prompt patch)

notes.subject: File ISS for missing WOFF2 font payload — site-wide body text falls back to Georgia
notes.body:
EXP-202 added @font-face declarations for Source Serif 4, Inter, and JetBrains Mono pointing to ./fonts/*.woff2, but the actual .woff2 payload files were never committed. docs/fonts/ contains only LICENSES.md; live site returns 404 for all three families. The visible symptom is site-wide body text silently falling back to Georgia — most obvious on plain

blocks like the Self-Contradictions tab intro paragraph, which the operator noticed on the live site. The EXP-202 commit message (450e7b8) explicitly flagged this as a manual TODO: 'WOFF2 files need manual download — see docs/fonts/LICENSES.md. Font fallbacks to Georgia/system-ui/Consolas until WOFF2 files are committed.' That TODO was never actioned. Action: file an ISS for the missing WOFF2 payload. Remediation: download the three OFL-licensed font families per docs/fonts/LICENSES.md (Source Serif 4 variable or 400/600/700; Inter variable or 400/500/600/700; JetBrains Mono variable or 400/700) from Google Fonts or the upstream repos, subset to Latin if size-sensitive, commit the .woff2 files under docs/fonts/ with the filenames the @font-face declarations expect. notes.author: operator (steve)
notes.subject: File ISS — decider reports Phase A EXPs as blocked after they were integrated; source-of-truth confusion
notes.body:
SYMPTOM
daily-report-2026-04-23T04-24.json contains, under blocked_items and recommended_actions (priority 2):

    'All 8 design-refresh Phase A EXPs (EXP-202 through EXP-213) are DRAFT_AWAITING_HUMAN_GROUP_REVIEW with explicit integration_blocked_until gates. No integration action taken. Operator needs to post HNOTE-DESIGN-REFRESH-PHASE-A-INTEGRATION-APPROVED-NNN to release them.'

This is false. Phase A1 was integrated 2026-04-22T~20:30Z (commits 450e7b8 + efffeb2 + cleanup e7efc30). HNOTE-DESIGN-REFRESH-PHASE-A-INTEGRATION-APPROVED-001 and its SUPPLEMENT are in human-notes.json with status=consumed. EXP-211 is correctly held for Phase A2, which APPROVED-001-SUPPLEMENT pre-conditionally approves. The recommended operator action is a no-op — the HNOTE it asks for already exists and was consumed.

GROUND TRUTH — monitor/analyst/expansion-tracker.json rows:
    EXP-202 → status: operator-approved, integrated: True
    EXP-207 → status: operator-approved, integrated: True
    EXP-208 → status: operator-approved, integrated: True
    EXP-209 → status: operator-approved, integrated: True
    EXP-210 → status: operator-approved, integrated: True
    EXP-212 → status: operator-approved, integrated: True
    EXP-213 → status: operator-approved, integrated: True
    EXP-211 → status: revised, integrated: None (held for Phase A2, conditionally pre-approved)

SUSPECTED ROOT CAUSE — source-of-truth confusion.
The append-only EXP files (monitor/analyst/expansions/EXP-NNN.json) carry their original analyst-authored metadata indefinitely:
    'status': 'DRAFT_AWAITING_HUMAN_GROUP_REVIEW'
    'integration_blocked_until': 'operator posts HNOTE-DESIGN-REFRESH-PHASE-A-INTEGRATION-APPROVED-NNN ...'
Per CLAUDE.md ownership rules, EXP files are append-only — nobody rewrites them once analyst creates them. So their 'status' and 'integration_blocked_until' fields capture the state at the MOMENT OF CREATION, not the live state. The authoritative live state is expansion-tracker.json's 'integrated' + 'status' fields, which decider itself updates on integration.

Decider's blocked-items scanner appears to be reading EXP file metadata directly (or some derivative) rather than deriving blocked status from tracker.integrated != True.

EVIDENCE THAT THIS IS PERSISTENT, NOT A ONE-OFF STALE CACHE
5 daily reports contain the 'DRAFT_AWAITING_HUMAN_GROUP_REVIEW' phrase, most recently 2026-04-23T04-24 (post-integration). Earlier occurrences (2026-04-21T12-30, 20-33, 2026-04-22T10-31, 16-35) were all pre-integration and possibly accurate at the time. The 04-24 report is the clear regression.

COUNTER-HYPOTHESIS TO RULE OUT
Maybe the decider code path that writes blocked_items was reading a snapshot from before the pull --rebase completed, and this is just a stale-read race. Audit for this possibility — if decider's clone was stale on the 04-24 run, tracker would also have looked stale, but tracker's 'last_updated' is 2026-04-22T21:10:07Z (post-integration), so decider COULD have seen the correct integrated flags. This reduces confidence that stale-read is the cause and increases confidence that the scanner is looking at the wrong file.

REQUESTED ACTION
1. File an ISS for the decider blocked-items / recommended-actions scanner, tagged source-of-truth-drift.
2. Audit the decider code path (or prompt language) that produces daily_report.blocked_items and recommended_actions. Identify where 'DRAFT_AWAITING_HUMAN_GROUP_REVIEW' and 'integration_blocked_until' are derived. If it reads EXP files, fix to instead derive blocked status from tracker.items[i] with rule: blocked iff tracker row has integrated != True AND integration_blocked_until is non-empty AND no consumed APPROVED HNOTE references this EXP.
3. As a defensive measure, add a sanity check: if blocked_items references EXP-NNN but tracker shows integrated: True for that EXP, log a warning and suppress the item.
4. Priority: not urgent — the operator noticed and handled it manually. Backlog. Fine to handle after the operator returns from travel.

NOT IN SCOPE
Do not rewrite the analyst-authored EXP files to flip their status — they're append-only by design and the whole point of the tracker is to maintain the mutable layer on top of immutable analyst artifacts.
notes.consumption_note: Filed ISS-1533 to track source-of-truth-drift in blocked-items scanner. Backlog priority per operator direction.

notes.author: operator (steve)
notes.subject: ISS-1425 disposition: operator agrees with curmudgeon on all three EXP-211-redesign findings. Revise before integration.
notes.body:
DECISION: Operator sides with curmudgeon on ISS-1425. All three findings in the EXP-211-redesign curmudgeon review are valid and must be addressed before integration. Per operator: 'seem like valid things.'

PER-FINDING DISPOSITION

Finding #1 (MAJOR — dropped self-serving denominator sentence): ALREADY RESOLVED. The sentence 'The headline number is manually entered with a self-serving denominator.' was re-inserted via HNOTE-EXP-211-REDESIGN-REINSERT-HEADLINE-SENTENCE-001 and landed at commit 3e2d24b. Verified present in build-scripts/generate-html.js at the Overview sc-card currently rendering 'Honest accuracy: X, not Y'. Mark this finding CLOSED on next curmudgeon pass. No further action.

Finding #2 (MODERATE — <aside> landmark-skip risk): FIX REQUIRED. The proposed <aside> semantic element for the accuracy-math sidebar creates a real accessibility regression. Screen readers using landmark navigation (VoiceOver rotor, JAWS regions, NVDA D-key) will offer users a shortcut to skip <aside> elements, causing them to miss the load-bearing 'manually entered with a self-serving denominator' critique. This is truth-surface-area loss for users with assistive tech. Resolution options — analyst picks the one that fits the revision best: (a) use <div> with a heading structure instead of <aside>, so the critique remains in document flow; (b) keep <aside> but ensure it is NOT skip-landmarked — add role="region" with aria-label and a visible h3 so content is navigable, not skippable; (c) keep accuracy math inline as it currently is and skip the sidebar demotion entirely. Curmudgeon's preferred resolution per review file: minor_edit that preserves critique reachability. Do NOT ship with bare <aside>.

Finding #3 (MODERATE — one-scroll → two-scroll reachability regression): FIX REQUIRED. Demoting the accuracy math to a sidebar pushes the hardcoded-number critique below the fold on common viewports, making it reachable in two scrolls instead of one. This is surface-area loss on a load-bearing critique. Resolution: the revised EXP-211 must preserve one-scroll reachability on ~1080p/1440p desktop and on typical mobile viewports. Analyst to verify in the revision with a concrete viewport check (state the viewport tested in the EXP file's acceptance_criteria). Acceptable approaches: (a) keep accuracy math in the Overview flow but visually demoted (smaller type, muted color, still above fold); (b) sidebar placement that stays above fold on desktop and mobile via layout strategy (sticky, positioned before the fold, etc.); (c) drop the sidebar demotion and handle the 'audit-number vs headline-number' hierarchy via typography weight / color rather than positional demotion.

ADDITIONAL CURMUDGEON REQUIREMENT (from review file, not an ESCALATE finding but explicitly blocking_for_integration): Add --ink-3 contrast acceptance criterion to the revised EXP. Specifically, the acceptance_criteria list should include a WCAG 2.1 AA contrast check for the demoted accuracy math's text color against its background. Target: ≥ 4.5:1 for body text, ≥ 3:1 for large text. Verify in the revision, state the measured ratio in acceptance_criteria, not just the target.

PIPELINE DISPOSITION
1. Decider: close ISS-1425 as 'operator-sided-with-curmudgeon, revision required'. Reference this HNOTE in the closure note.
2. Decider: assign a new analyst task for EXP-211-redesign-v2 (or whatever the append-only-safe revision ID is) that addresses findings #2 and #3 and adds the --ink-3 contrast criterion. Source of revision guidance: this HNOTE's per-finding resolution notes above. Curmudgeon's recommended_action was 'minor_edit' so this should not require a from-scratch redraft — just targeted patches to the proposed redesign.
3. Analyst writes the revised expansion, decider files it into expansions/, curmudgeon re-reviews under the same HNOTE-DESIGN-REFRESH-PHASE-A2-INTEGRATION-APPROVED-CONDITIONAL-001 rubric.
4. If curmudgeon's re-review is clean (no new ESCALATE findings), integration proceeds automatically per the conditional approval — no additional operator HNOTE needed.

NOT BLOCKING OPERATOR
This HNOTE resolves ISS-1425's pending-human state. Operator does not need to be in the loop on the revision itself unless curmudgeon surfaces new ESCALATE findings in the re-review.

URGENCY: Normal. The 7 Phase A1 EXPs are already live; Phase A2 (EXP-211-redesign) shipping a few days later is fine and arguably better — lets the design refresh land in visible stages rather than as one big flip.
notes.consumption_note: ISS-1425 closed as wontfix pending analyst revision. EXP-211-redesign-v2 task filed to analyst via HNOTE-EXP-211-REDESIGN-V2-TASK-001. Three curmudgeon findings addressed: #1 already resolved, #2+#3 require analyst revision.

notes.author: operator (steve)
notes.subject: STOP the 'update CLAUDE.md PAT' self-propagating recommendation. CLAUDE.md has no PAT and never did.
notes.body:
FORENSICS

The decider's daily reports (2026-04-21 ×2, 2026-04-23T12-28, 2026-04-23T17-30) have escalated 'Update CLAUDE.md PAT' to Priority #1 / immediate for three days, citing a stale PAT string (github_pat_11AQFYCRA0vYz...) allegedly stored in CLAUDE.md. Operator investigated 2026-04-23 and found this is a phantom.

EVIDENCE

1. grep of CLAUDE.md for any PAT pattern: zero hits. Run: grep -E 'github_pat_|ghp_|token|credential|Devilwench' CLAUDE.md → no output.
2. FUSE and clone CLAUDE.md md5-identical (7caa81c513c47df50e47a5ee3ffc4903).
3. git log -S "AQFYCRA0vYz" -- CLAUDE.md → empty. That PAT string has NEVER been in CLAUDE.md in any commit in repo history. The only tracked files containing any PAT pattern are the decider's own past daily reports.
4. No PAT in SESSION-CONTEXT.md, any monitor/prompts/*.md, .gitignore, or any tracked file outside decider reports.

MECHANISM (how the phantom self-propagates)

monitor/status.json contained two fields — pat_rotation_note and a 'PAT note:' sentence in notes_this_run — asserting 'Devilwench PAT still in CLAUDE.md (now revoked). Update needed.' Every decider run reads status.json at start as state-carrier. The decider trusted the assertion, faithfully wrote it forward into its new daily report's recommended_actions, and the next decider read THAT report as context and repeated the cycle. Three days, no one re-verified.

ROOT CAUSE

The 2026-04-22 PAT rotation was a real event (Devilwench PAT 403'd, operator provided new PAT). In the remediation checklist someone (possibly the decider itself while writing status.json) added 'Update CLAUDE.md + scheduled task prompts' as a TODO item. This was incorrect from the start — CLAUDE.md never stored a PAT and the scheduled-task prompts only EXTRACT the PAT at runtime (via grep -oP 'x-access-token:\K[^@]+' from workspace .git/config). The remediation step was phantom from the moment it was written.

ACTIONS TAKEN BY OPERATOR

1. monitor/status.json: removed pat_rotation_note and the 'PAT note:' sentence from notes_this_run. Replaced with pat_note_resolved_by_operator containing the forensic summary. This breaks the feedback loop.
2. Filing this HNOTE.

DIRECTIVES FOR DECIDER

1. Do NOT re-raise 'update CLAUDE.md PAT' as a recommended_action without first running: grep -E 'github_pat_|ghp_' CLAUDE.md and confirming at least one hit. Zero hits = no action needed, do not escalate.
2. If you read a stale TODO in status.json or a prior daily report that asserts a file contains a string, verify the file actually contains the string before propagating the claim. Fact-carrying forward without re-verification is how this phantom lasted three days.
3. If you find this HNOTE's directive inconvenient because the state genuinely changed (operator did rotate PAT into CLAUDE.md for some reason), that is fine — re-raise with concrete evidence (a quoted grep hit from CLAUDE.md) rather than narrative assertion.

STATUS: open. Close after next decider run confirms it no longer carries the phantom recommendation.
notes.superseded_reason: Over-broad claim — operator only checked project CLAUDE.md; user-level CLAUDE.md (outside operator session visibility) did contain a Devilwench PAT. See CORRECTED-002 for full retraction.

notes.author: operator (steve, via cowork session)
notes.subject: SUPERSEDES HNOTE-DECIDER-CLAUDE-MD-PAT-PHANTOM-001 — corrected diagnosis: PAT WAS in user-level CLAUDE.md (operator-only, KEV-scoped). Operator removed it.
notes.body:
RETRACTION & CORRECTION

HNOTE-DECIDER-CLAUDE-MD-PAT-PHANTOM-001 (filed 2026-04-23 by operator) asserted that 'CLAUDE.md has no PAT and never has' based on grep of the project CLAUDE.md and its git history. That claim was OVER-BROAD. The decider's session loads CLAUDE.md from a hierarchy — project-level (the file in the repo) AND user-level (~/CLAUDE.md or ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md, which lives outside the project repo). Operator's session for the cowork investigation could not read the user-level file (Permission denied on those paths) and missed it.

WHAT WAS ACTUALLY TRUE

1. Project CLAUDE.md (FUSE + operator clone, md5 7caa81c513c47df50e47a5ee3ffc4903) had no PAT. That part was correctly verified.
2. User-level CLAUDE.md DID contain a Devilwench PAT, scoped semantically to the KEV project. The decider correctly identified it as a KEV-scoped PAT and used the dome FUSE .git/config PAT for dome operations. The decider's behavior was technically correct documentation, not a phantom.
3. The 'Update CLAUDE.md PAT' recommended_action that has appeared in decider daily reports for ~4 days was substantively pointing at a real situation — just one the project-level grep didn't surface.

OPERATOR RESOLUTION (2026-04-25)

Operator confirmed the Devilwench PAT was already expired/invalid and removed it from user-level CLAUDE.md. The user-level CLAUDE.md no longer contains the PAT or any references to it. Each project's scheduled tasks rely on FUSE .git/config injection (dome and KEV both), so removing the literal PAT from CLAUDE.md does not break either project's automation.

CORRECTED DIRECTIVE FOR DECIDER

1. The 'Update CLAUDE.md PAT' recommended_action is no longer applicable. Source removed.
2. If a future session detects a PAT in any CLAUDE.md (project OR user-level), document it once, do not re-escalate every run, and flag for operator review. Don't accumulate the recommendation across runs.
3. Apologies on behalf of operator for the over-broad earlier HNOTE — it was a good-faith but incomplete investigation. The decider's repeated flagging was not phantom.

STATUS.JSON CORRECTION

The pat_note_resolved_by_operator field in monitor/status.json has been updated to reflect the corrected understanding (it previously claimed 'ZERO hits' which was true for project but misleading overall).

DECIDER ACTION REQUIRED: read this HNOTE, mark consumed. The previous PAT-PHANTOM-001 HNOTE has been marked superseded; do not re-process it.

notes.subject: PAT genuinely has contents:write — retract earlier 'PAT scope insufficient' diagnoses, treat 403 as transient/environmental
notes.body:
DIAGNOSTIC CORRECTION

The decider's push-failure log at monitor/integrity/push-failure-2026-04-25T17-28.json claimed: 'Fine-grained PAT may lack contents:write scope.' That claim is WRONG. Operator's rescue-push commit c8af541 also said 'LONG-TERM FIX REQUIRED ... scheduled-task FUSE-injected PAT has read-only access' — also wrong.

EMPIRICAL VERIFICATION (operator, 2026-04-25T18:30Z)

1. SHA256 of operator-clone PAT == SHA256 of FUSE remote-URL PAT (b9587d031cad...). They are byte-identical.
2. PAT authenticates as 'Devilwench' (login) on GitHub API.
3. POST to /repos/funwithscience-org/dome-model-review/issues/99999999/comments returns HTTP 404 (issue does not exist) — not 403. This means write authorization was granted; the issue lookup just failed. If contents:write were missing, GitHub would have returned 403 before checking issue existence.
4. Fresh clone via FUSE auth URL, fresh commit by Claude Opus 4.7 / noreply@anthropic.com, push → SUCCEEDED (test commit f79e788, immediately reverted via force-push to c8af541).
5. Operator's rescue-push c8af541 itself succeeded with the same PAT.

CONCLUSION: the PAT has contents:write. The decider's 17:28Z 403 was transient — most likely a GitHub-side hiccup or a per-session network/IP-state quirk that has since cleared.

DIRECTIVES FOR DECIDER (and any pushing agent)

1. When `git push` returns 403, wait 30 seconds and retry ONCE before declaring failure. The decider's self-apply procedure has been updated (decider-patches-and-selfapply.md, retry-on-403 wrapper) to do this automatically.
2. When logging push-failure (after retry), do NOT write 'PAT lacks contents:write' or 'PAT scope issue.' The PAT does have write. The cause is environmental/transient/non-PAT.
3. The push-failure log should include `files_synced_to_fuse: true` so operator can rescue-push from cowork session.
4. Do NOT add 'update CLAUDE.md PAT' or 'rotate PAT' to recommended_actions on a 403. The PAT works.

WHY THIS MATTERS

The phantom 'CLAUDE.md PAT stale' recommendation lasted ~4 days before operator caught it (HNOTE-DECIDER-CLAUDE-MD-PAT-CORRECTED-002). A new phantom — 'scheduled-task PAT lacks contents:write' — would self-propagate the same way if not killed at the source. This HNOTE plus the decider prompt change kill it preemptively.

SEPARATELY (not a directive — just truth):

The Devilwench account does have push access to dome-model-review (the operator is funwithscience-org and Devilwench is a collaborator/PAT owner there). When a 'Devilwench' identity gets denied, it's a per-attempt transient or per-session environmental issue, not an account-level access revocation.

STATUS: pending.

notes.title: Universal-pusher mode: workspace-sync now rescues decider 403 push failures
notes.body:
Operator directive 2026-04-26 ~07:21Z: "for the moment, let's make workspace universal pusher. otherwise we'll be rescuing decider all day."

Two changes shipped together (this commit):

1. monitor/prompts/reference/decider-patches-and-selfapply.md — push-failure block now copies committed-but-unpushed files to FUSE via [git diff --name-only origin/main...HEAD] before exiting. Logs include universal_pusher_rescue.rescued_count and rescued_files array. Decider message updated to say "Operator does NOT need to manually rescue-push."

2. monitor/prompts/workspace-sync.md — replaced ~24-entry OWNED_BY_GIT direction-guard array with smaller NEVER_PUSH deny-list. Files MOVED OUT (now eligible for workspace-sync push, mtime-guarded only): data/wins.json, data/sections.json, data/predictions.json, data/uncounted-failures.json, monitor/decisions/open-issues.json, monitor/decisions/closed-issues.json, monitor/curmudgeon/priority-queue.json. Files KEPT IN deny-list: docs/* (build.js artifacts), build-scripts/*, build.js, test.js, CLAUDE.md, monitor/v6-restructure-map.json, monitor/integrity/workspace-sync-skips.jsonl (circular if pushed back), monitor/curmudgeon/pending-digest.json (generated in clone), monitor/decisions/prop-009-enforce.flag (semantic flag), monitor/prompts/*.md.

Added sync iteration over monitor/decisions/applied-patches/*.json (subdir not previously walked).

Also updated build.js OWNERSHIP comments (1.1 docstring + workspace-sync-skips.jsonl rationale) and CLAUDE.md File Ownership Rules (renamed reference from OWNED_BY_GIT to NEVER_PUSH; added universal-pusher mode callout block).

Flow under new design:
  T+0  Decider commits in clone, push 403s, copies committed files to FUSE via the new rescue block, exits.
  T+~30m  Workspace-sync (hourly at :03) sees FUSE files newer than git, pushes them.
  T+~30m+1s  Pipeline state caught up — no operator action required.

Safety nets:
  - smart_copy mtime guard still protects against stale FUSE overwriting fresh git.
  - cmp -s short-circuit still prevents identical-content pushes.
  - NEVER_PUSH deny-list still blocks build-artifact / source-code round-trips.
  - workspace-sync.md self-tests catch deny-list misclassification at startup.

Rollback if needed: revert the workspace-sync.md change first (decider rescue copy is idempotent and harmless if nothing reads from it).
notes.acceptance: Decider acknowledges new universal-pusher behavior in its next run note. No action required beyond awareness — the changes self-activate on next workspace-sync and next decider push attempt.
